message.content:
You are confirmer #1, independent. Confirm a production-hardening fix is BOTH appropriate (correct, no regression, idiomatic) AND complete (fully closes it; for a code change the named regression test must DISCRIMINATE — fail if the fix were reverted). Repo: /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay
Fix G-015 in .github/workflows/ci.yml
What: SBOM step applies the same .pip-audit-ignore allowlist so an accepted advisory doesn't fail SBOM generation.
Test(s): test_sbom_step_applies_the_allowlist
Steps: read the actual changed file(s); read the test and judge whether it discriminates; check no regression. For a doc/refactor item, judge correctness/coverage rather than revert-discrimination. Verdict CONFIRMED (both true) or CONCERN (something missing/wrong/under-tested). Be skeptical and specific.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: I'll verify the G-015 fix. Let me start by examining the changed file and the test.

input.command: cd /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay && git log --oneline -8 && echo "---" && ls .pip-audit-ignore 2>/dev/null; echo "==="; git log -p -3 -- .github/workflows/ci.yml | head -200
input.description: Show recent commits and ci.yml history

content.content:
7dccfd8 fix(hardening-p4): batch E — egress, error contracts, PII & test discrimination (8 findings)
c49261e fix(hardening-p4): batch D — CI / supply-chain reproducibility (G-015,016,022)
2eea958 fix(hardening-p4): batch C — provenance, observability & resample memory (G-009,010,012,023)
91dfb1a fix(hardening-p4): batch B — concurrency, reasoning-tier & serialization (G-002,003,004)
d61bd4e fix(hardening-p4): batch A — methodology integrity (G-001,005,006,011,017)
0788961 docs(hardening): pass-3 merged (#161); open pass-4 with retrospective dimensions
54cd88c Merge pull request #161 from [REDACTED]/harden/pass-3
5f16960 docs(hardening): record pass-3 (49/54 confirmed, all fixed/reclassified) + pass-4 dimensions
---
===
commit c49261ea2b0e689c319bc92ffa4523b674dec840
Author: [REDACTED] <[REDACTED]>
Date:   Wed Jun 17 00:00:47 2026 -0400

    fix(hardening-p4): batch D — CI / supply-chain reproducibility (G-015,016,022)
    
    Pass-4 confirmed findings (2-agent verified, revert-discriminated):
    
    - G-015 (medium) the SBOM step applies the SAME allowlist as the pip-audit gate. pip-audit
      exits non-zero on ANY finding regardless of output format, so under set -euo pipefail an
      ACCEPTED (allowlisted) advisory would fail SBOM generation even though the gate passed —
      the gate runs first, so a non-allowlisted advisory has already failed the job.
    - G-022 (low) pip-audit (==2.10.1) and pip-licenses (==5.5.5) are version-pinned so the
      supply-chain gate is reproducible run-to-run (a tooling bump can't silently flip
      advisory/license results — the analogue of the uv pin #F-031).
    - G-016 (medium) documented + scripted the lockfile regeneration Dependabot can't do
      itself (scripts/regenerate_lockfiles.sh, uv pinned to the CI version) and pointed
      dependabot.yml at it, so a Dependabot PR isn't permanently stuck on the stale-lock gate.
      (An auto-commit workflow was considered and rejected: dependabot's read-only token /
      pull_request_target write-token risks can't be validated here.)
    
    Gates: ruff + ruff format --check + mypy --strict clean; 512 passed, 5 skipped.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <[REDACTED]>

diff --git a/.github/workflows/ci.yml b/.github/workflows/ci.yml
index 91309f3..7b04a94 100644
--- a/.github/workflows/ci.yml
+++ b/.github/workflows/ci.yml
@@ -94,7 +94,11 @@ jobs:
         with:
           python-version: "3.12"
       - name: Install pip-audit
-        run: python -m pip install --upgrade pip pip-audit
+        # Pin the audit tooling to exact versions (#G-022): an unpinned pip-audit can change
+        # advisory results / exit behavior between releases, making the supply-chain gate
+        # non-reproducible (a green commit could fail tomorrow on a tooling bump) — the analogue
+        # of the uv pin (#F-031).
+        run: python -m pip install --upgrade pip && python -m pip install pip-audit==2.10.1
       - name: Lockfiles are in sync with pyproject (reproducibility guard)
         run: |
           # Pin uv to the exact version the lockfiles were generated with (#F-031): an unpinned
@@ -132,7 +136,19 @@ jobs:
           # downstream license/transitive monitoring has a stable artifact to track (#147). No
           # `|| true` mask (#F-012): a pip-audit failure must fail the step, and the SBOM must be
           # non-empty, so a broken/absent SBOM cannot pass CI green.
-          pip-audit --progress-spinner off -r requirements.lock --format cyclonedx-json -o sbom.cyclonedx.json
+          # Apply the SAME allowlist as the gate (#G-015): pip-audit exits non-zero on ANY finding
+          # regardless of output format, so without the allowlist an ACCEPTED advisory would fail
+          # SBOM generation here even though the gate step passed — coupling SBOM emission to vuln
+          # status and making the allowlist ineffective. The gate step already ran first, so a
+          # NON-allowlisted advisory has failed the job before we reach here.
+          IGNORES=()
+          if [ -f .pip-audit-ignore ]; then
+            while IFS= read -r id || [ -n "$id" ]; do
+              case "$id" in ''|\#*) continue ;; esac
+              IGNORES+=(--ignore-vuln "$id")
+            done < .pip-audit-ignore
+          fi
+          pip-audit --progress-spinner off -r requirements.lock --format cyclonedx-json -o sbom.cyclonedx.json "${IGNORES[@]}"
           test -s sbom.cyclonedx.json
           echo "SBOM emitted ($(wc -c < sbom.cyclonedx.json) bytes)"
       - name: Upload SBOM artifact
@@ -151,7 +167,7 @@ jobs:
           # lockfile pins (#F-013). Advisory monitoring of the rest is handled by Dependabot.
           pip install --require-hashes -r requirements.lock
           pip install --no-deps -e .
-          python -m pip install pip-licenses
+          python -m pip install pip-licenses==5.5.5  # pinned for reproducible license output (#G-022)
           pip-licenses --format=json --with-system > licenses.json
           python scripts/license_gate.py licenses.json
       - name: Upload license report

commit 8608da47f3ec3317eb85602c8cb555938354cb73
Author: [REDACTED] <[REDACTED]>
Date:   Tue Jun 16 22:07:49 2026 -0400

    fix(hardening-p3): batch 3 — CI / supply-chain hardening (8 findings)
    
    Pass-3 confirmed findings (2-agent verified, revert-discriminated):
    
    - F-004 test lanes install hash-pinned: both `core` (new requirements-core.lock,
      dev extra only — preserves the ADR-0006 no-backends guard) and `integration`
      (requirements.lock) now `pip install --require-hashes -r <lock>` + `--no-deps -e .`,
      so tests execute against exactly the audited/SBOM'd versions, closing the window
      where a malicious patch release within a pyproject range could run under test.
    - F-012 SBOM step no longer masks failure with `|| true`; the upload errors (not
      warns) on a missing file — a broken/absent SBOM can't pass CI green.
    - F-013 license gate scans the hash-pinned lockfile set, not a live `pip install -e
      .[all]` that could resolve different versions than pip-audit audited.
    - F-014 new `all-checks` umbrella job depends on [core, integration, audit]; branch
      protection requires only it, so audit/integration can't be dropped from the gate.
    - F-015 license gate denies SSPL/EUPL/AGPL PROSE forms ("Server Side Public License",
      "European Union Public Licence", "Affero") — the short tokens alone let those
      families bypass. Replaced the string-presence test with a behavioral one (#F-039).
    - F-031 uv pinned (uv==0.11.21) in the lockfile sync check so a uv format change can't
      produce spurious stale-lock diffs blocking every PR.
    - F-037 all GitHub Actions pinned to 40-char commit SHAs (tag as comment); a mutable
      tag force-push can't inject code into CI.
    - F-050 license_gate.py opens the report via a context manager (no leaked fd).
    
    Also added requirements-core.lock + its CI sync check (#F-031), and test guards for
    F-052 (allowlist filename pinned in ci.yml) and F-053 (dependabot github-actions
    ecosystem asserted).
    
    Gates: ruff + ruff format --check + mypy --strict clean; 486 passed, 5 skipped.
    F-015 revert-discrimination verified.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <[REDACTED]>

diff --git a/.github/workflows/ci.yml b/.github/workflows/ci.yml
index 9aaeacd..91309f3 100644
--- a/.github/workflows/ci.yml
+++ b/.github/workflows/ci.yml
@@ -14,14 +14,19 @@ jobs:
       matrix:
         python-version: ["3.11", "3.12", "3.13", "3.14"]
     steps:
-      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
-      - uses: actions/setup-python@v5
+      - uses: actions/checkout@34e114876b0b11c390a56381ad16ebd13914f8d5 # v4
+      - uses: actions/setup-python@a26af69be951a213d495a4c3e4e4022e16d87065 # v5
         with:
           python-version: ${{ matrix.python-version }}
-      - name: Install (no extras)
+      - name: Install (no extras, hash-pinned)
         run: |
+          # Install the dev toolchain from the hashed, audited core lockfile so tests run against
+          # EXACTLY the versions pip-audit scanned and the SBOM describes — not a live PyPI
+          # resolution where a malicious patch release could execute under test (#F-004). The
+          # core lock carries only the `dev` extra (no backends), preserving the ADR-0006 guard.
           python -m pip install --upgrade pip
-          pip install -e ".[dev]"
+          pip install --require-hashes -r requirements-core.lock
+          pip install --no-deps -e .
       - name: Lint (ruff)
         run: ruff check src tests
       - name: Format check (ruff)
@@ -55,14 +60,18 @@ jobs:
       # loopback host keeps the data-sovereignty guard satisfied; creds are redacted on error
       ASSAY_TEST_POSTGRES_URL: postgresql://postgres:postgres@localhost:5432/assay
     steps:
-      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
-      - uses: actions/setup-python@v5
+      - uses: actions/checkout@34e114876b0b11c390a56381ad16ebd13914f8d5 # v4
+      - uses: actions/setup-python@a26af69be951a213d495a4c3e4e4022e16d87065 # v5
         with:
           python-version: "3.12"
-      - name: Install (all extras)
+      - name: Install (all extras, hash-pinned)
         run: |
+          # Install from the hashed all-extras lockfile so the integration suite executes against
+          # exactly the audited, pinned versions — closing the window where a compromised patch
+          # release within a pyproject range could run during the real-backend tests (#F-004).
           python -m pip install --upgrade pip
-          pip install -e ".[dev,reasoning,observability,persistence,orchestration,baseline]"
+          pip install --require-hashes -r requirements.lock
+          pip install --no-deps -e .
       - name: Type-check against real backend types (mypy)
         run: mypy
       # Real backends now run: langgraph graph + interrupt/resume, mlflow lifecycle, OTel,
@@ -80,19 +89,27 @@ jobs:
   audit:
     runs-on: ubuntu-latest
     steps:
-      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
-      - uses: actions/setup-python@v5
+      - uses: actions/checkout@34e114876b0b11c390a56381ad16ebd13914f8d5 # v4
+      - uses: actions/setup-python@a26af69be951a213d495a4c3e4e4022e16d87065 # v5
         with:
           python-version: "3.12"
       - name: Install pip-audit
         run: python -m pip install --upgrade pip pip-audit
-      - name: Lockfile is in sync with pyproject (reproducibility guard)
+      - name: Lockfiles are in sync with pyproject (reproducibility guard)
         run: |
-          python -m pip install --upgrade uv
+          # Pin uv to the exact version the lockfiles were generated with (#F-031): an unpinned
+          # `uv` upgrade can change output format/ordering/annotations and produce spurious diffs
+          # that fail this gate on every PR even when the lockfiles are current.
+          python -m pip install uv==0.11.21
           uv pip compile pyproject.toml --all-extras --universal --generate-hashes -o /tmp/requirements.regen.lock
           # ignore the auto-generated header (timestamp/command line) when diffing
           diff <(grep -v '^#' requirements.lock) <(grep -v '^#' /tmp/requirements.regen.lock) \
             || { echo "::error::requirements.lock is stale — run: uv pip compile pyproject.toml --all-extras --universal --generate-hashes -o requirements.lock"; exit 1; }
+          # the core lockfile (dev extra only) backs the hash-pinned core lane (#F-004) and must
+          # stay in sync too.
+          uv pip compile pyproject.toml --extra dev --universal --generate-hashes -o /tmp/requirements-core.regen.lock
+          diff <(grep -v '^#' requirements-core.lock) <(grep -v '^#' /tmp/requirements-core.regen.lock) \
+            || { echo "::error::requirements-core.lock is stale — run: uv pip compile pyproject.toml --extra dev --universal --generate-hashes -o requirements-core.lock"; exit 1; }
       - name: pip-audit (reproducible known-vulnerability scan)
         run: |
           set -euo pipefail
@@ -112,30 +129,56 @@ jobs:
         run: |
           set -euo pipefail
           # Emit a machine-readable software bill of materials for the pinned dependency set so
-          # downstream license/transitive monitoring has a stable artifact to track (#147).
-          pip-audit --progress-spinner off -r requirements.lock --format cyclonedx-json -o sbom.cyclonedx.json || true
-          test -s sbom.cyclonedx.json && echo "SBOM emitted ($(wc -c < sbom.cyclonedx.json) bytes)"

1	name: ci
2	
3	on:
4	  push:
5	    branches: [main]
6	  pull_request:
7	
8	jobs:
9	  # Dependency-free core: the engine must lint, type-check, and test with NO extras installed
10	  # (optional backends are import-guarded; integration tests skip). This guards ADR-0006.
11	  core:
12	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
13	    strategy:
14	      matrix:
15	        python-version: ["3.11", "3.12", "3.13", "3.14"]
16	    steps:
17	      - uses: actions/checkout@34e114876b0b11c390a56381ad16ebd13914f8d5 # v4
18	      - uses: actions/setup-python@a26af69be951a213d495a4c3e4e4022e16d87065 # v5
19	        with:
20	          python-version: ${{ matrix.python-version }}
21	      - name: Install (no extras, hash-pinned)
22	        run: |
23	          # Install the dev toolchain from the hashed, audited core lockfile so tests run against
24	          # EXACTLY the versions pip-audit scanned and the SBOM describes — not a live PyPI
25	          # resolution where a malicious patch release could execute under test (#F-004). The
26	          # core lock carries only the `dev` extra (no backends), preserving the ADR-0006 guard.
27	          python -m pip install --upgrade pip
28	          pip install --require-hashes -r requirements-core.lock
29	          pip install --no-deps -e .
30	      - name: Lint (ruff)
31	        run: ruff check src tests
32	      - name: Format check (ruff)
33	        run: ruff format --check src tests
34	      - name: Type-check (mypy)
35	        run: mypy
36	      - name: Test (pytest)
37	        run: pytest -q
38	
39	  # Extras lane: install the real backends and run the integration suite so the backend code
40	  # paths actually execute, and mypy type-checks against the REAL (not ignored) library types.
41	  # Service-dependent tests (ollama/qdrant/postgres) skip when the service is absent in CI; the
42	  # extra-only tests (langgraph graph, mlflow lifecycle, OTel, numpy primitives, methodology
43	  # e2e) run for real.
44	  integration:
45	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
46	    services:
47	      postgres:
48	        image: postgres:16
49	        env:
50	          POSTGRES_PASSWORD: postgres
51	          POSTGRES_DB: assay
52	        ports: ["5432:5432"]
53	        options: >-
54	          --health-cmd "pg_isready -U postgres"
55	          --health-interval 5s --health-timeout 5s --health-retries 10
56	      qdrant:
57	        image: qdrant/qdrant:v1.12.4
58	        ports: ["6333:6333"]
59	    env:
60	      # loopback host keeps the data-sovereignty guard satisfied; creds are redacted on error
61	      ASSAY_TEST_POSTGRES_URL: postgresql://postgres:postgres@localhost:5432/assay
62	    steps:
63	      - uses: actions/checkout@34e114876b0b11c390a56381ad16ebd13914f8d5 # v4
64	      - uses: actions/setup-python@a26af69be951a213d495a4c3e4e4022e16d87065 # v5
65	        with:
66	          python-version: "3.12"
67	      - name: Install (all extras, hash-pinned)
68	        run: |
69	          # Install from the hashed all-extras lockfile so the integration suite executes against
70	          # exactly the audited, pinned versions — closing the window where a compromised patch
71	          # release within a pyproject range could run during the real-backend tests (#F-004).
72	          python -m pip install --upgrade pip
73	          pip install --require-hashes -r requirements.lock
74	          pip install --no-deps -e .
75	      - name: Type-check against real backend types (mypy)
76	        run: mypy
77	      # Real backends now run: langgraph graph + interrupt/resume, mlflow lifecycle, OTel,
78	      # numpy primitives, the T3 drain/classification (synthetic SDK, no metered call), AND —
79	      # via the service containers — the live Postgres checkpointer + Qdrant vector store.
80	      # Ollama and the metered frontier tier are not in CI and skip honestly.
81	      - name: Integration + unit tests with coverage
82	        run: pytest -q --cov=assay_engine --cov-report=term-missing
83	
84	  # Supply-chain: fail on known-vulnerable dependencies (a security/privacy engine must not ship
85	  # with advisories). The scan runs against the committed, fully-pinned, hashed lockfile
86	  # (requirements.lock) so the audit is reproducible across runs and matches what installs
87	  # (#108, #130, #131, #132, #145, #146). An allowlist file (.pip-audit-ignore, one advisory id
88	  # per line, '#' comments and blank lines allowed) covers accepted/unfixable ones.
89	  audit:
90	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
91	    steps:
92	      - uses: actions/checkout@34e114876b0b11c390a56381ad16ebd13914f8d5 # v4
93	      - uses: actions/setup-python@a26af69be951a213d495a4c3e4e4022e16d87065 # v5
94	        with:
95	          python-version: "3.12"
96	      - name: Install pip-audit
97	        # Pin the audit tooling to exact versions (#G-022): an unpinned pip-audit can change
98	        # advisory results / exit behavior between releases, making the supply-chain gate
99	        # non-reproducible (a green commit could fail tomorrow on a tooling bump) — the analogue
100	        # of the uv pin (#F-031).
101	        run: python -m pip install --upgrade pip && python -m pip install pip-audit==2.10.1
102	      - name: Lockfiles are in sync with pyproject (reproducibility guard)
103	        run: |
104	          # Pin uv to the exact version the lockfiles were generated with (#F-031): an unpinned
105	          # `uv` upgrade can change output format/ordering/annotations and produce spurious diffs
106	          # that fail this gate on every PR even when the lockfiles are current.
107	          python -m pip install uv==0.11.21
108	          uv pip compile pyproject.toml --all-extras --universal --generate-hashes -o /tmp/requirements.regen.lock
109	          # ignore the auto-generated header (timestamp/command line) when diffing
110	          diff <(grep -v '^#' requirements.lock) <(grep -v '^#' /tmp/requirements.regen.lock) \
111	            || { echo "::error::requirements.lock is stale — run: uv pip compile pyproject.toml --all-extras --universal --generate-hashes -o requirements.lock"; exit 1; }
112	          # the core lockfile (dev extra only) backs the hash-pinned core lane (#F-004) and must
113	          # stay in sync too.
114	          uv pip compile pyproject.toml --extra dev --universal --generate-hashes -o /tmp/requirements-core.regen.lock
115	          diff <(grep -v '^#' requirements-core.lock) <(grep -v '^#' /tmp/requirements-core.regen.lock) \
116	            || { echo "::error::requirements-core.lock is stale — run: uv pip compile pyproject.toml --extra dev --universal --generate-hashes -o requirements-core.lock"; exit 1; }
117	      - name: pip-audit (reproducible known-vulnerability scan)
118	        run: |
119	          set -euo pipefail
120	          # --strict: any dependency that cannot be collected/audited FAILS the gate (no silent
121	          # skips). --require-hashes: every pin must carry a hash. Build --ignore-vuln args as an
122	          # array so allowlist entries can't word-split or glob-expand, and read the file
123	          # newline-agnostically so the last line is honored even without a trailing newline.
124	          IGNORES=()
125	          if [ -f .pip-audit-ignore ]; then
126	            while IFS= read -r id || [ -n "$id" ]; do
127	              case "$id" in ''|\#*) continue ;; esac
128	              IGNORES+=(--ignore-vuln "$id")
129	            done < .pip-audit-ignore
130	          fi
131	          pip-audit --strict --require-hashes --progress-spinner off -r requirements.lock "${IGNORES[@]}"
132	      - name: SBOM (CycloneDX) from the lockfile
133	        run: |
134	          set -euo pipefail
135	          # Emit a machine-readable software bill of materials for the pinned dependency set so
136	          # downstream license/transitive monitoring has a stable artifact to track (#147). No
137	          # `|| true` mask (#F-012): a pip-audit failure must fail the step, and the SBOM must be
138	          # non-empty, so a broken/absent SBOM cannot pass CI green.
139	          # Apply the SAME allowlist as the gate (#G-015): pip-audit exits non-zero on ANY finding
140	          # regardless of output format, so without the allowlist an ACCEPTED advisory would fail
141	          # SBOM generation here even though the gate step passed — coupling SBOM emission to vuln
142	          # status and making the allowlist ineffective. The gate step already ran first, so a
143	          # NON-allowlisted advisory has failed the job before we reach here.
144	          IGNORES=()
145	          if [ -f .pip-audit-ignore ]; then
146	            while IFS= read -r id || [ -n "$id" ]; do
147	              case "$id" in ''|\#*) continue ;; esac
148	              IGNORES+=(--ignore-vuln "$id")
149	            done < .pip-audit-ignore
150	          fi
151	          pip-audit --progress-spinner off -r requirements.lock --format cyclonedx-json -o sbom.cyclonedx.json "${IGNORES[@]}"
152	          test -s sbom.cyclonedx.json
153	          echo "SBOM emitted ($(wc -c < sbom.cyclonedx.json) bytes)"
154	      - name: Upload SBOM artifact
155	        if: always()
156	        uses: actions/upload-artifact@ea165f8d65b6e75b540449e92b4886f43607fa02 # v4
157	        with:
158	          name: sbom-cyclonedx
159	          path: sbom.cyclonedx.json
160	          if-no-files-found: error
161	      - name: License gate (deny strong-copyleft in the pinned dependency set)
162	        run: |
163	          set -euo pipefail
164	          # Fail if any dependency carries a strong-copyleft license incompatible with the
165	          # Apache-2.0 distribution (#147). Scan the SAME hash-pinned set pip-audit audited, not a
166	          # live `pip install -e .[all]` resolution that could pull different versions than the
167	          # lockfile pins (#F-013). Advisory monitoring of the rest is handled by Dependabot.
168	          pip install --require-hashes -r requirements.lock
169	          pip install --no-deps -e .
170	          python -m pip install pip-licenses==5.5.5  # pinned for reproducible license output (#G-022)
171	          pip-licenses --format=json --with-system > licenses.json
172	          python scripts/license_gate.py licenses.json
173	      - name: Upload license report
174	        if: always()
175	        uses: actions/upload-artifact@ea165f8d65b6e75b540449e92b4886f43607fa02 # v4
176	        with:
177	          name: license-report
178	          path: licenses.json
179	          if-no-files-found: warn
180	
181	  # Single required status check (#F-014): branch protection should require ONLY `all-checks`.
182	  # It fans in every other job, so adding a new job automatically gates merges without anyone
183	  # remembering to update the branch-protection rule — and `audit`/`integration` can never be
184	  # silently dropped from the merge gate by requiring just `core`.
185	  all-checks:
186	    if: always()
187	    needs: [core, integration, audit]
188	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
189	    steps:
190	      - name: Verify all required jobs succeeded
191	        run: |
192	          results="${{ needs.core.result }} ${{ needs.integration.result }} ${{ needs.audit.result }}"
193	          echo "job results: $results"
194	          for r in $results; do
195	            if [ "$r" != "success" ]; then
196	              echo "::error::a required job did not succeed ($r)"
197	              exit 1
198	            fi
199	          done
200	          echo "all required jobs succeeded"
201

1	"""Supply-chain / CI audit-gate regression guards (pass-2 #130, #131, #132, #145, #146, #147).
2	
3	These guard configuration that lives in .github/workflows/ci.yml and the committed lockfile.
4	The CI YAML cannot be unit-imported, so the guards are twofold:
5	  1. config-level: assert the hardened invocation is present in ci.yml (a revert to the buggy
6	     form fails the test);
7	  2. behavioral: run the exact allowlist-parsing bash loop against fixtures to prove it is
8	     newline-agnostic and comment/blank tolerant (the original `while read -r id` form drops a
9	     final line that lacks a trailing newline).
10	"""
11	
12	from __future__ import annotations
13	
14	import shutil
15	import subprocess
16	from pathlib import Path
17	
18	import pytest
19	
20	_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent
21	_CI = _ROOT / ".github" / "workflows" / "ci.yml"
22	_LOCK = _ROOT / "requirements.lock"
23	
24	# The hardened allowlist loop, lifted verbatim from ci.yml's audit job. Kept here so the
25	# behavioral test exercises the SAME logic the workflow runs.
26	_PARSE_LOOP = r"""
27	set -euo pipefail
28	IGNORES=()
29	if [ -f "$1" ]; then
30	  while IFS= read -r id || [ -n "$id" ]; do
31	    case "$id" in ''|\#*) continue ;; esac
32	    IGNORES+=(--ignore-vuln "$id")
33	  done < "$1"
34	fi
35	printf '%s\n' "${IGNORES[@]:-<none>}"
36	"""
37	
38	# The ORIGINAL buggy loop, for the revert-discrimination assertion in the behavioral test.
39	_PARSE_LOOP_BUGGY = r"""
40	IGNORES=""
41	if [ -f "$1" ]; then
42	  while read -r id; do [ -n "$id" ] && IGNORES="$IGNORES --ignore-vuln $id"; done < "$1"
43	fi
44	printf '%s\n' "$IGNORES"
45	"""
46	
47	
48	def _ci_text() -> str:
49	    return _CI.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
50	
51	
52	def test_pip_audit_runs_strict_and_require_hashes():
53	    # #130: without --strict an unauditable dependency is silently skipped while CI stays green.
54	    # --require-hashes ties the scan to the pinned, hashed lockfile.
55	    text = _ci_text()
56	    assert "pip-audit --strict --require-hashes" in text, (
57	        "audit gate must run pip-audit with --strict --require-hashes (#130)"
58	    )
59	
60

230	    text = _ci_text()

232	    assert "needs: [core, integration, audit]" in text
233	
234	
235	def test_ci_pins_uv_version():
236	    # #F-031: uv must be pinned so a format change between versions cannot cause spurious stale-lock
237	    # diffs that block every PR.
238	    text = _ci_text()
239	    assert "pip install uv==" in text, "uv is not pinned in the lockfile sync check (#F-031)"
240	    assert "pip install --upgrade uv\n" not in text, "unpinned uv upgrade reintroduced"
241	
242

244	    # #G-015: the SBOM pip-audit (which exits non-zero on ANY finding) must apply the same
245	    # allowlist as the gate, or an ACCEPTED advisory fails SBOM generation under set -euo pipefail

258	    text = _ci_text()
259	    assert "pip-audit==" in text, "pip-audit not version-pinned (#G-022)"
260	    assert "pip-licenses==" in text, "pip-licenses not version-pinned (#G-022)"
261	    assert "install --upgrade pip pip-audit\n" not in text, (
262	        "unpinned pip-audit install reintroduced"

265	
266	def test_dependabot_lockfile_regen_is_documented():
267	    # #G-016: Dependabot can't regenerate the uv lockfiles, so the manual regen path must be
268	    # documented + scripted, or every Dependabot PR is stuck on the stale-lock gate.
269	    regen = _ROOT / "scripts" / "regenerate_lockfiles.sh"

content.text: That confirms an important detail: on older bash (pre-4.4), `"${IGNORES[@]}"` with an empty array under `set -u` is an unbound-variable error. But GitHub Actions `ubuntu-latest` ships bash 5.x. Let me verify whether the local bash is old (which would explain the error) versus what CI uses, and that the gate step uses the identical idiom (already in production, so this is consistent precedent).

content.text:
The local bash is 3.2 (macOS), which is why the empty-array expansion errored. GitHub Actions `ubuntu-latest` ships bash 5.x, where `"${IGNORES[@]}"` with an empty array under `set -u` is safe (the empty-array-under-nounset fix landed in bash 4.4). The pip-audit gate step (lines 124-131) already uses this exact idiom and is in production/passing CI, so the SBOM step uses an established, proven-correct pattern on the target runner. No new risk introduced.

Summary of findings:
- The fix in `/Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/.github/workflows/ci.yml` (lines 144-151) builds the `IGNORES` array from `.pip-audit-ignore` identically to the gate step and appends `"${IGNORES[@]}"` to the SBOM `pip-audit` invocation. This correctly decouples SBOM emission from accepted-advisory status while preserving the property that a non-allowlisted advisory has already failed the job in the prior gate step.
- Idiomatic: reuses the exact, already-hardened parsing loop (newline-agnostic, comment/blank tolerant, no word-split/glob via array). Comment accurately explains the rationale.
- No regression: full suite (30 tests) passes; `set -euo pipefail` empty-array expansion is safe on the ubuntu-latest bash 5.x runner, matching the existing gate step.
- The test `test_sbom_step_applies_the_allowlist` discriminates: reverting the `"${IGNORES[@]}"` arg from the SBOM call makes it fail (confirmed), and it passes when restored. It scopes its assertions to the SBOM step slice (`text[index("SBOM (CycloneDX)") : index("Upload SBOM artifact")]`), so it genuinely guards the SBOM step and not the gate step.

input.reasoning:
The fix (ci.yml lines 144-151) adds the same .pip-audit-ignore allowlist-parsing loop to the SBOM step and appends "${IGNORES[@]}" to the SBOM pip-audit invocation, identical to the already-hardened gate step (lines 124-131). This correctly stops an ACCEPTED/allowlisted advisory from failing SBOM generation (pip-audit exits non-zero on any finding regardless of --format) under set -euo pipefail, while the prior gate step still fails the job on any non-allowlisted advisory before this step runs. The loop reuses the proven newline-agnostic, comment/blank-tolerant, array-based (no word-split/glob) pattern — idiomatic and consistent with precedent.

No regression: full test_supply_chain.py suite (30 tests) passes. The empty-array expansion "${IGNORES[@]}" under set -u errors only on bash <4.4 (e.g. the local macOS bash 3.2), but GitHub Actions ubuntu-latest ships bash 5.x where it is safe, and the identical idiom is already in the production gate step — no new risk.

Discrimination verified: removing "${IGNORES[@]}" from the SBOM pip-audit line (the pre-fix form) makes test_sbom_step_applies_the_allowlist FAIL (asserts the literal cyclonedx-json -o sbom.cyclonedx.json "${IGNORES[@]}" plus --ignore-vuln and .pip-audit-ignore within the SBOM-step slice); restoring it passes. The test scopes its assertions to the SBOM step substring (between "SBOM (CycloneDX)" and "Upload SBOM artifact"), so it genuinely guards the SBOM step, not the gate. Both appropriate and complete.
